# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Google Compute Engine instances. Use default service account and default disk encryption settings for quick deployment. Focus on getting instances running - we'll add custom encryption keys and security hardening in phase 2.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

resources:
- name: projects/myproject
  type: cloud_identity.googleapis.com/Project
  
# Define the default service account for instances, which has "roles/"viewer" role granting it access to view project resources and permissions on all disks & images in GCE (default instance template). Adjust with your specific requirements or use custom identity if required as per gcloud documentation.  Replace `my-service@example.com` by the user email you want for this service account
parameters:
# Service Account ID, can be provided using parameter substitution like '${params('default_sa')}'. This will ensure same deployment in a different project or region does not need redefinition of default SA   Replace `my-service@example.com` by the user email you want for this service account
  default_sa: my-service@example.com | gcloud config set compute/default_service_account Email  || "ServiceAccount:" || $(data.values['deploymentManager']['resources'][0].properties.'email')     |  echo -n  >> ~/.gcloud ; cat  ~/.gcloud 
# The name of the zone in which to deploy resources, for example: us-central1 or europe-west1 regions and zones like e. gce instances are regionless default compute engine instance set this value as per your requirement    Replace `us-central1` by suitable project location   #europe
  zone_location: us-central1    |  echo -n >> ~/.gcloud; cat  ~/.gcloud  || "zone:" || $(data.values['deploymentManager']['resources'][0].properties.'name') | xargs    echoxec tgz .zones  
# The name of the custom encryption key to use, if none specified then no cryptographic security measures will be applied on all disks using default service account user must provide a customer managed Encryption Key for disk level encryption or specify `customer_managed_encryption` as per your requirement  Replace  `projects/my-projecrts/locations/us-central1/keys/defaultKey' by suitable Customer ManageEncrypt key ID   
   customerManagedKeys: projects/my-projcts/locations/us-centerl1/keys/defaultexamplekey|gcloud compute disks create myDisk --zone=$(params('zone_location')) –project="$PROJECTID"-size=50GB     -H=--noauth_local_web_proxy
